pred_reason: The SWC-105 label is reasonable as the registerMintedToken function lacks access control, allowing arbitrary address array manipulation. This directly enables uncontrolled growth of the addresses array through repeated external calls, matching the SWC-105 vulnerability pattern. Evidence: registerMintedToken has no access control (Finding 1); addresses.push(addr) in registerTokenInternal (Finding 5); User-controlled array length via external function (Finding 5)

SWC-101 addresses integer underflow in user array indexing, while SWC-107 covers reentrancy vulnerabilities in external calls. These align with findings showing underflow risks in _beforeTokenTransfer and reentrancy issues in state-changing functions. Evidence: Task 7 finding: Critical underflow vulnerability in user array indexing; Task 1 finding: Confirmed reentrancy vulnerability in contract functions; Task 6 finding: Arithmetic operation issues despite Solidity 0.8.x protections

SWC-101 (Integer Overflow and Underflow) is reasonable because the findings include multiple instances of arithmetic vulnerabilities. The SafeMath ceil function contains potential integer overflow and division by zero issues, while the incorrect percentage calculation and balance deductions also create arithmetic risks. Evidence: SafeMath.ceil function contains potential integer overflow vulnerability; Incorrect percentage calculation in findOnePercent causes 10x token loss; Transfer functions incorrectly deduct full amount creating accounting errors
